titleStmt.title: Telegram from Alfred Byrne to Mrs. Walter Harris Wilson, 23 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alfred Byrne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A telegram (with envelope) from Alfred (‘Alfie’) Byrne (1882–1956), Irish Parliamentary Party (IPP) MP for Dublin Harbour, to Mrs. Walter Harris Wilson (or Katherine Anna (‘Katie’) Gifford Wilson (1875–1957)), informing her that the Under-Secretary for War will recommend that the authorities in Ireland stop the deportation of women.After the death of her husband in the 1918 influenza epidemic, Katherine Wilson returned to Ireland permanently (from Wales) and became active in Sinn Féin and Cumann na mBan. Katherine was sister to Muriel Enid Gifford MacDonagh (1884–1917) - who married Thomas MacDonagh (1878–1916); Grace (Evelyn) Gifford (1888–1955) - who married Joseph Mary Plunkett (1887–1916) in Kilmainham Gaol hours before his execution; Helen Ruth (‘Nellie’) Gifford Donnelly (1880–1971) - who took part in the Easter Rising; and Sydney Madge (‘John Brennan’) Czira (Gifford) (1889–1974) - who submitted articles to Arthur Griffith's newspaper, 'Sinn Fein'.

msIdentifier.collection: Allen Library, Br. Allen Collection, IE/AL/1916/53/1-2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
POST OFFICE TELEGRAPHS: Office of Origin, House of Commons Handed in at 4.17 Received here at 5.34
TO{ Wilson 7 Bushfield Park Fairview Dublin Under secretary for war informed
me in House he will
recommend to authorities in Ireland
the advisability of stopping female
deportments Byrne
TELEGRAM
NO CHARGE FOR DELIVERY. Mr Walter Harris Wilson
Temple Villas.
